title: Lab: Repair a Relief-Service Bundle Selector
summary: Lab: Repair a Relief-Service Bundle Selector retrieves prior skills in an original unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css form focus, css form errors, css form native custom in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 1: use a color-only background change under hover and focus because both states mean selection.
Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 1: create a distinct focus-visible indicator, reserve unclipped space, reinforce groups carefully, and test keyboard plus forced-color paths.
Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 1: clip overflow on every field group so focus rings cannot change card dimensions.

options.text: Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 3: red borders alone provide clear error recovery.

Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 3: reduce disabled control opacity until its label and value are no longer readable.

options.text: Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 5: custom checkboxes need only a hidden native input and decorative box.

Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 5: start with the native control, use accent-color or bounded appearance changes, and customize only with complete semantic and behavioral evidence.
Unfamiliar selection state and forced-color incident case 5: replace select with a list of div elements because options are only visual rows.
